text: The video shows adhesions and scar tissue surrounding the gastric band, indicating areas where the band has eroded into the stomach tissue.
The surgeon uses an electrocautery and a harmonic scalpel to carefully dissect through the adhesions, clearing the path for further surgical access.
The surgeon follows the tube to locate the gastric band and begins to open the surrounding tissue to access the stomach for band removal.
The surgeon exposes the buccal portion of the gastric band, visible within the stomach wall, indicating its penetration into the gastric tissue.
The surgeon locates the buckle of the gastric band within the stomach wall and prepares to cut it loose, facilitating its removal from the stomach interior.
The surgeon observes the dark brown color of the gastric band, indicating it has been exposed to stomach acid and shows signs of chemical burn.
The surgeon introduces a suction device into the surgical field in anticipation of encountering stomach content during the procedure.
The surgeon has removed the band from the stomach, leaving a large visible hole in the stomach wall.
The surgeon stitches up the hole in the stomach wall using sutures, closing the opening left by the removal of the band.
The surgeon has closed the hole in the stomach using sutures, with the stitches visibly securing the tissue together.
The surgeon places the contaminated band into a sterile bag to prevent contact with the abdominal wall and incision, preparing to remove it from the abdominal cavity.
The surgeon places a drain into the surgical site as a precautionary measure.
